relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The disciples ask about greatness in heaven’s reign.
Jesus places a child at the center.
Entrance requires turning into childlike humility.
Greatness is redefined as lowliness.
Receiving the little one receives Jesus.
Harming the trusting little ones carries grave weight.
Stumbling-blocks are real, but the one causing them is warned.
Anything that causes deep stumbling must be dealt with radically.
The eye itself must yield if it blocks life.
The little ones are not to be despised; their heavenly messengers behold the Father’s face.
A later manuscript note echoes the saving of the lost.
The shepherd seeks the one wandering sheep.
Finding the lost one brings overflowing joy.
The Father does not will the loss of little ones.
Restoration begins privately, seeking to regain the sibling.
Witnesses help establish truth when hearing fails.
The assembly becomes the final place of appeal.
Binding and loosing link communal discernment with heaven and earth.
Agreement in prayer opens shared request before the Father.
Where two or three gather into his name, Jesus is present.
Peter asks for the limit of release.
Jesus expands forgiveness beyond calculation.
The reign is pictured as a king settling accounts.
A vast impossible debt is brought forward.
The debt threatens the whole household.
The servant begs for patience.
Compassion releases the person and the debt.
The released servant refuses to release a smaller debt.
The same plea is spoken back to him.
He chooses confinement over mercy.
The community grieves the contradiction.
The lord names the failure to mirror received release.
Mercy received must become mercy given.
Refused mercy becomes consequence.
Release must happen from the heart, not merely the lips.
